definition: EM sovereign stress operates through common-cause exposure to shared vulnerabilities (pegged FX, current account deficits, USD debt, low reserves) rather than sequential contagion. Countries lacking these vulnerabilities are unaffected regardless of proximity. Falsified across four major episodes: Tequila 1994, Asia 1997-98, Argentina 2001, Taper Tantrum 2013.
evidence: Historical falsification across four major EM episodes consistently supports common-cause over contagion
Countries sharing external vulnerabilities hit simultaneously by common shocks; others unaffected
open_questions: Does the common-cause framework hold for scenarios involving the US itself as the source of stress?
How should EM portfolios be constructed to hedge common-factor vs. idiosyncratic risk?
